mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines below 4.5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 101.5 signals robust manufacturing activity that drives corporate profit growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO rises above 101.5 over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to produce positive 60d SPX returns in 60%+ of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.4 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that increases equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS rises above 4.5% over 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) below 320 combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates stable pricing environments that support equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined decline in CPIAUCSL and rise in RSAFS fails to produce positive 20d SPX returns in 60%+ of cases, or their joint signal correlation drops below 0.3 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 160,000 combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates strong labor market dynamics that drive consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS > 160,000 and UNRATE < 4.4% fails to produce positive 20d SPX returns in 60%+ of cases, or their interaction effect loses significance (p > 0.05) in rolling 3-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 324 combined with stagnant monetary policy (FEDFUNDS < 3.9%) signals central bank inaction against price pressures, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of CPIAUCSL > 324 and FEDFUNDS < 3.9% fails to produce negative 60d SPX returns in 60%+ of cases, or the interaction term loses significance (p > 0.05) in rolling 3-year regressions.
